sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 6.8 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.6)

statement.latex: Can a residually finite locally normal group be embedded in a Cartesian product of finite groups in such a way that each element of the group has at most finitely many central projections? { Yu.\,M.\,Gorchakov} Yes, it can (M.\,J.\,Tomkinson, Bull. London Math. Soc., { 13} (1981), 133--137).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
